agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order cancellation is a core e-commerce/logistics capability that allows customers to reverse a purchase before fulfillment is complete. It intersects order management, payment/refund processing, inventory, and potentially shipping/logistics domains. The PM statement is extremely terse — 'cancel their order' leaves nearly every business rule undefined.","bounded_context":"Order Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Order","definition":"A confirmed customer purchase comprising one or more line items, tracking a lifecycle from placement through fulfillment or cancellation."},{"term":"Order Cancellation","definition":"A customer- or system-initiated action that terminates an order, halting further fulfillment and triggering any applicable refund or reversal processes."},{"term":"Cancellation Window","definition":"The time period or set of order states during which cancellation is permitted. Undefined in current spec."},{"term":"Order Status","definition":"The current lifecycle state of an order (e.g., Placed, Confirmed, Processing, Shipped, Delivered, Cancelled). Exact states are system-specific."},{"term":"Refund","definition":"The return of payment to the customer following a cancellation, subject to refund policy rules."},{"term":"Partial Cancellation","definition":"Cancelling individual line items within a multi-item order rather than the entire order."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A cancelled order cannot transition to any state other than Cancelled. Cancellation is a terminal state.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing a cancelled order to be re-activated would corrupt order state, potentially double-charge customers or ship goods without payment.","testable_assertion":"Given an order in status Cancelled, no API call or system event can change its status to any other value."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Only the customer who placed the order (the order owner) may initiate cancellation of that order.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing arbitrary users to cancel other users' orders is a data integrity and authorization violation.","testable_assertion":"Given User A owns Order 123, when User B attempts to cancel Order 123, then the system rejects the request with an authorization error."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"An order may only be cancelled while it is in a cancellable state. Orders that have been shipped or delivered are not cancellable through this flow.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The exact set of cancellable states is a business policy decision — a VP could extend or narrow the cancellation window. However, the principle that some states are non-cancellable is near-universal in logistics.","testable_assertion":"Given an order in status Shipped, when the customer requests cancellation, then the system rejects the request and indicates the order is no longer cancellable."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A cancellation request must record the reason for cancellation, the requesting user, and the timestamp.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Audit trail integrity — financial and regulatory compliance requires knowing who cancelled what and when.","testable_assertion":"Given a successful cancellation, the system persists cancellation_reason, cancelled_by, and cancelled_at fields, all non-null."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"When an order is cancelled, any associated payment must be reversed or refunded according to the applicable refund policy.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Refund timing, method, and completeness are policy decisions (immediate vs. delayed, full vs. partial with restocking fee), but the principle that cancellation triggers a refund process is a business truth.","testable_assertion":"Given a paid order that is successfully cancelled, then a refund process is initiated within the system."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer has a confirmed order in status 'Placed' or 'Processing'","when":"The customer requests to cancel that order","then":"The order status transitions to 'Cancelled', a cancellation record is persisted with reason/user/timestamp, and a refund process is initiated"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer has an order in status 'Shipped' or 'Delivered'","when":"The customer requests to cancel that order","then":"The system rejects the cancellation with a clear message explaining the order is no longer eligible for cancellation and suggests the returns process instead"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A customer has an order that is already in status 'Cancelled'","when":"The customer requests to cancel that order again","then":"The system returns an idempotent success or a clear message that the order is already cancelled, without creating duplicate cancellation records or duplicate refunds"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"User A owns an order and User B is authenticated","when":"User B attempts to cancel User A's order","then":"The system rejects the request with a 403 Forbidden and no state change occurs on the order"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A customer has a confirmed order in a cancellable state","when":"The customer requests cancellation but the payment refund fails","then":"The order is still marked as Cancelled, the failed refund is logged, and an alert is raised for manual resolution — the customer is not left in an inconsistent state"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A customer has a multi-item order in a cancellable state","when":"The customer requests to cancel the entire order","then":"All line items are cancelled and the full payment amount is refunded (partial cancellation behavior is undefined in current spec)"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An order is in a cancellable state and a concurrent fulfillment process advances the order to 'Shipped'","when":"The customer submits a cancellation request at nearly the same time","then":"Exactly one outcome wins: either the order is cancelled OR it is shipped — the system never produces a state where the order is both cancelled and shipped"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A customer views their order history","when":"They look at a cancelled order","then":"The order clearly displays its Cancelled status, the cancellation reason, and the refund status"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Race condition: customer cancels while warehouse ships simultaneously","expected_behavior":"The system enforces optimistic locking or equivalent concurrency control so that exactly one outcome wins. If shipment already occurred, cancellation is rejected. If cancellation wins, shipment is halted or recalled."},{"scenario":"Double-cancel: customer clicks cancel button twice rapidly","expected_behavior":"The operation is idempotent. The second request either succeeds silently (already cancelled) or returns a clear 'already cancelled' response. No duplicate refunds are issued."},{"scenario":"Payment refund fails after order is cancelled","expected_behavior":"Order remains in Cancelled state (not rolled back to active). A failed-refund flag is set and an alert is raised for manual intervention. Customer is notified that refund is being processed."},{"scenario":"Order with mixed fulfillment states (multi-item: some shipped, some not)","expected_behavior":"Behavior is currently undefined. This is a key ambiguity — does the system allow partial cancellation of un-shipped items while shipped items proceed? Needs PM clarification."},{"scenario":"Guest checkout user attempts to cancel","expected_behavior":"Undefined — does the system support guest orders? If so, identity verification for cancellation must be addressed (e.g., email link, order number + email combo)."},{"scenario":"Order has applied promotions, coupons, or loyalty points","expected_behavior":"Refund calculation must account for promotional pricing. Coupons/points must be restored or handled per refund policy. This is undefined in current spec."},{"scenario":"Cancellation requested during a payment processing hold (authorized but not captured)","expected_behavior":"System should void the authorization rather than process a refund, avoiding unnecessary charge-then-refund cycles."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Cancellation directly affects revenue recognition, refund processing costs, and potentially payment processor fees. A poorly implemented cancellation flow (e.g., duplicate refunds, missing refunds) creates direct financial loss. Overly restrictive cancellation hurts customer lifetime value.","operational":"Cancellation must integrate with inventory (restock), fulfillment (halt picking/packing), shipping (intercept if possible), and finance (refund). Each integration point is a failure mode. The race condition between cancellation and fulfillment is the highest operational risk.","user":"Cancellation is a trust-building feature. Customers who can easily cancel are more likely to place orders. Poor cancellation UX (unclear eligibility, missing confirmation, opaque refund status) erodes trust and drives support tickets.","risk":"Primary risks: (1) duplicate refunds from concurrency bugs, (2) orders stuck in inconsistent states, (3) regulatory non-compliance if cancellation rights are not respected (e.g., EU consumer cooling-off periods). Without clear spec, each of these is likely to be implemented incorrectly.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Gain ability to self-service cancel, reducing friction. Poor implementation increases support contacts."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Should see reduced ticket volume for cancellation requests. Needs tooling to handle edge cases (failed refunds, post-ship cancellations)."},{"group":"Finance/Accounting","impact":"Must handle refund reconciliation. Needs audit trail for all cancellations."},{"group":"Warehouse/Fulfillment","impact":"Must receive and act on cancellation signals before shipping. Race condition handling directly affects their workflow."},{"group":"Payment/Billing","impact":"Must support void (pre-capture) and refund (post-capture) flows triggered by cancellation."}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"The spec is a single sentence with no defined cancellation window, no state machine, no refund policy, no mention of partial cancellation, no authorization model, and no edge case guidance. Nearly every business rule had to be inferred from general e-commerce/logistics domain knowledge rather than explicit requirements. Multiple critical policy decisions remain unmade.","high_confidence_areas":["Cancellation is a terminal state (universal domain truth)","Only order owners can cancel their own orders (authorization invariant)","Audit trail is required for cancellations (compliance baseline)","Shipped/delivered orders cannot be cancelled via this flow (logistics reality)"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact set of cancellable order states — depends on system's state machine","Refund policy (full, partial, restocking fee, timing)","Whether partial cancellation of line items is supported","Guest user cancellation identity verification","Promotional/coupon/loyalty point handling on cancellation","Regulatory requirements (EU cooling-off period, regional consumer protection laws)","Whether cancellation reason is required or optional from the user"],"assumptions":["The system has a defined order lifecycle with discrete statuses","Payment has been collected or authorized at the time of cancellation","The feature is for customer-initiated cancellation (not admin/system cancellation)","The system has authenticated users with order ownership relationships"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"No cancellation window or eligible states defined. 'Cancel their order' does not specify WHEN cancellation is allowed. Is it only before processing? Before shipping? Up to N hours after placement? The entire cancellation policy hinges on this definition.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Warehouse/Fulfillment","Customer Support"],"options":["Allow cancellation only in 'Placed' status (most restrictive)","Allow cancellation up to 'Processing' (moderate)","Allow cancellation any time before 'Shipped' (most permissive)","Time-based window (e.g., 30 minutes after placement)"],"recommendation":"Allow cancellation in any state before 'Shipped', as this is the most customer-friendly policy that remains operationally feasible. Require PM to confirm."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Refund policy is entirely unspecified. Does cancellation trigger a full refund always? Are there restocking fees? What about shipping costs already incurred? What is the refund method and timeline?","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Finance/Accounting","Payment/Billing"],"options":["Full refund always","Full refund minus restocking fee","Full refund only if cancelled before processing, partial after","Refund to original payment method vs. store credit"],"recommendation":"Default to full refund to original payment method for customer trust. PM and Finance must confirm."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Partial cancellation (individual line items) is not addressed. For multi-item orders, can a customer cancel some items but keep others? This significantly affects refund calculation and fulfillment logic.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Warehouse/Fulfillment","Finance/Accounting"],"options":["Only full-order cancellation supported in v1","Partial cancellation supported from day one"],"recommendation":"Ship v1 with full-order cancellation only. Add partial cancellation as a fast-follow once refund proration logic is defined."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"No specification of whether cancellation reason is required from the customer, or what the allowed reasons are. This affects UX flow and analytics value.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Product/Analytics"],"options":["Reason required from a predefined list","Reason optional with free-text","No reason collected"],"recommendation":"Require selection from a predefined reason list (changed mind, found cheaper, ordered by mistake, delivery too slow, other) with optional free-text. Provides analytics value without blocking the cancellation."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}

content.thinking:
The PM says "Users should be able to cancel their order." This is quite vague. Let me analyze this from a domain perspective and produce my full output schema.

This is ambiguous - many details are missing about cancellation rules, time windows, refund policies, etc. But I need to produce acceptance criteria for what IS clear while escalating what's ambiguous.

Let me classify: This is `ambiguity` - the requirement can be interpreted multiple ways. "Cancel their order" is vague about timing, conditions, refund handling, partial cancellation, etc.
